title: lycium californicum subsp. carinatum
intro: Lycium californicum subsp. carinatum, also known as the California desert box or desert box, is a subspecies of the Lycium californicum plant, a type of shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as arthritis, diabetes, and hypertension. The health benefits of Lycium californicum subsp. carinatum include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against cell damage. Lycium californicum subsp. carinatum can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are often used to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
